title: 10 best herbal capsules for cirrhosis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with cirrhosis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active compound, silymarin, has been shown to reduce liver damage caused by cirrhosis by protecting the liver cells from oxidative stress and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and preventing liver cell death, milk thistle may help slow or even reverse the progression of cirrhosis, improving liver function and overall health. This natural supplement offers a promising adjunct therapy for managing this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle seeds that has been shown to protect liver cells from damage and promote regeneration, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.

Antioxidant activity: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver tissue and contribute to the progression of cirrhosis, making it an effective natural treatment for liver disease.

Liver-protective compounds: The silymarin and flavonoids present in milk thistle capsules have been shown to prevent or reduce liver injury caused by toxins, such as those found in certain medications or substances, which can exacerbate cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the milk thistle plant contain high levels of silymarin, a potent antioxidant that helps protect the liver from damage and promotes its regenerative processes.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain a unique mixture of flavonoids, particularly silibinin, which has been shown to inhibit liver damage caused by toxins and reduce inflammation in the liver.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds and flowers from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Crush dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered milk thistle with three parts capsule filler in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal according to manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain a powerful antioxidant compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. Curcumin also improves liver function by reducing fibrosis and scar tissue formation, allowing the liver to heal and regenerate itself. Additionally, turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort associated with cirrhosis. Overall, taking herbal turmeric capsules regularly may help slow down the progression of cirrhosis and improve overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, such as curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
Cytotoxin inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids have been shown to inhibit the production of cytotoxins, such as TNF-alpha, that can further damage liver cells in people with cirrhosis.

Hepatoprotection: Turmeric's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help protect the liver from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially slowing or even reversing liver fibrosis and cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, a powerful antioxidant compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with liver damage.
Roots: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including polyphenols and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric root curcuma longa roots and wash thoroughly under running water.
Dry the roots in a food dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Grind 250g of dried turmeric root into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 500mg of turmeric powder with 200mg of gelatin capsule powder and 50mg of silicon dioxide for lubrication.
Fill the mixture into empty #0 capsules and store in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with cirrhosis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsule's active compound, berberine, has been shown to improve liver function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of cirrhosis. Additionally, barberry's natural detoxification properties help remove toxins from the liver, promoting its health and regenerative capabilities. This can help slow down or even reverse the progression of cirrhosis, ultimately improving overall liver function and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in barberry capsules, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting antioxidant activity, which can slow down cirrhosis progression.

Choleretic effect: Barberry capsules have choleretic properties, meaning they stimulate the contraction of the bile ducts and increase bile flow, which can help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis such as jaundice and pruritus (itching) by improving liver function and reducing bilirubin accumulation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in barberry capsules can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the development and progression of cirrhosis, by neutralizing reactive oxygen species and promoting cell survival.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve liver function in patients with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Contain high concentrations of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic properties that can help treat liver fibrosis.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried berberis vulgaris root bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 15 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with glycerin and water in a 1:3 ratio to create a liquid extract, allowing it to steep for at least 2 hours.
Strain the liquid extract through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids after squeezing out as much liquid as possible for about 30 minutes.

Transfer the liquid extract to a clean glass bottle and fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the extract per capsule using a capsule filler machine, allowing it to dry completely overnight.

Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place, checking for potency and quality after 6 months and discarding any that lose their effectiveness or develop an off-odor or color.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus capsules helps with cirrhosis because of its remarkable ability to promote liver health. The herb's unique properties have been shown to reduce inflammation, scarring, and oxidative stress in the liver, thereby slowing down the progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, Phyllanthus capsules have been found to stimulate the production of antioxidants, which help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals. By supporting liver function and reducing oxidative stress, Phyllanthus capsules may help alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis, such as fatigue, nausea, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Phyllanthus capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.

Hepatoprotective property: Phyllanthus has been shown to protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and chemicals, which can help prevent further liver damage and scarring associated with cirrhosis.

Immune-modulatory property: Phyllanthus capsules have immunomodulatory effects that may help regulate an overactive immune response in the liver, which is thought to contribute to inflammation and fibrosis in cirrhotic patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Phyllanthus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit liver fibrosis and reduce inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Phyllanthus contain saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the liver from damage caused by cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh phyllanthus amarus leaves and dry them under shade for two weeks.
Grind the dried phyllanthus leaves into fine powder using a blender or grinder for five minutes.
Mix 250 milliliters of distilled water with 50 grams of the powdered phyllanthus leaves in a saucepan and boil for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth into a clean container, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature.
Fill #00 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the cooled liquid extract and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain saponins, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, thereby slowing down the progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, dandelion root extract has been found to improve bile flow, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as jaundice and pruritus. Overall, incorporating dandelion capsules into a treatment plan may help to support liver health and manage the symptoms associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Bile production stimulation: Dandelion root stimulates the production of bile, which can help to improve liver function and reduce congestion in the liver and gallbladder, alleviating symptoms of cirrhosis.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce liver inflammation and scarring (fibrosis) caused by cirrhosis, improving overall liver function and reducing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bitter compounds, dandelion roots have been traditionally used to support liver function and alleviate symptoms associated with cirrhosis.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and flavonoids, dandelion seeds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to the development of cirrhosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves, ensuring they are free of pesticides and contaminants.
Dry the harvested materials in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 500g of dried dandelion root and 250g of dried dandelion leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered dandelion herbs with 100g of gelatin capsules in a bowl, filling each capsule to approximately 500mg per serving.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 12 months, ensuring proper labeling and storage conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties. Glycyrrhizin has been found to inhibit inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which are key factors in the development and progression of cirrhosis. Additionally, it has been reported to improve liver function and reduce liver damage by stimulating the production of antioxidants and modulating the immune response. As a result, herbal licorice capsules may be beneficial in managing the symptoms of cirrhosis and slowing its progression.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain antioxidants that help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which can slow down liver scarring and fibrosis progression in patients with cirrhosis.

Glycyrrhizin-based immunomodulatory effects: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, modulates the immune system's response to liver injury, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the healing process in cirrhotic livers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Barks: Licorice bark has been used in traditional medicine for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis such as fatigue and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a fine texture using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground licorice powder with 4 ounces of distilled water in a small saucepan and heat it over low flame for 10 minutes.
Simmer the mixture for an additional 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until a thick syrup forms and reduces to about 2 ounces.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool completely before pouring it into gelatin capsules using a dropper or spoon.
Fill each capsule with approximately 1/4 teaspoon of the cooled licorice syrup, sealing them tightly and storing them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain potent antioxidants that combat liver damage and inflammation, a hallmark of this chronic condition. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and scarring in the liver tissue, thereby improving its function and structure. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been shown to enhance liver detoxification, removing toxins and metabolic waste products that contribute to cirrhosis development. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal Asiatic pennywort capsules offer a natural solution for managing cirrhotic symptoms and promoting overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Asian Pennywort capsules have been shown to possess potent antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and liver damage associated with cirrhosis.
Inflammation reduction: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate inflammation in the liver, a common complication of cirrhosis, and prevent further tissue damage.

Hepatoprotective effects: Asian Pennywort has been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage caused by toxins, viruses, or other factors that contribute to cirrhosis development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of Asiatic pennywort are known to reduce inflammation and improve liver function, making them an effective part for treating cirrhosis.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain high levels of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic properties, helping to reverse fibrosis and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves from a trusted source within two hours of use.
Wash the harvested leaves gently in cold running water for ten minutes to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator set at 60°c for 6-8 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Grind the dried centella asiatica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered herb and seal immediately for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain active compounds that have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. The herbs in these capsules, such as Sambucus williamsii Hance, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help combat the oxidative stress caused by cirrhosis. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy liver tissue growth, Chinese thoroughwort capsules may slow down or even reverse liver damage, improving symptoms and quality of life for those with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chinese Thoroughwort capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a common symptom of cirrhosis and can further damage the organ.
Hepatoprotective property: The herb has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it helps protect the liver from further damage and promotes regeneration of damaged liver tissue.
Antioxidant property: Chinese Thoroughwort capsules contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and liver damage in cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate liver damage and improve liver function.
Rhizomes: Contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds that exhibit antifibrotic and antioxidant activities, helping to reduce liver fibrosis and scarring.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried bupleurum falcatum root powder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix the sifted powder with 60g of rice flour and 10g of cornstarch in a stainless steel bowl.
Add 300ml of distilled water and stir the mixture clockwise for 5 minutes to create a uniform paste.
Encapsulate the paste using #00 gelatin capsules and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Consume 2-3 capsules three times daily after meals for 6 months or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain Artemisia absinthium, a botanical that has been traditionally used to treat liver issues. The capsules' active compounds, including camphor and thujone, have been shown to stimulate bile production and improve liver function, which can help reduce inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and swelling, making it a promising natural supplement for supporting liver health in individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to cirrhosis. This property helps protect the liver from further damage.

Choleretic and cholekinetic properties: Wormwood stimulates the production of bile and improves its flow through the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis such as jaundice and pruritus (itching). By improving bile flow, wormwood may also reduce liver inflammation and scarring.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and fibrosis in the liver, which are common features of cirrhosis. This property may help slow or stop the progression of liver damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation associated with cirrhosis.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect the liver from further damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry artemisia absinthium leaves in the shade for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Crush 20 grams of dried wormwood leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part wormwood powder with three parts organic vegetable glycerin in a small bowl.
Allow the mixture to infuse for six hours or overnight in a cool dark place.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500-750mg of the infused wormwood-glycerin mixture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with cirrhosis because of their unique ability to promote liver health and repair damaged tissue. The capsules contain a compound called centella, which has been shown to improve liver function by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells, further supporting liver health and regeneration. By addressing the underlying causes of cirrhosis, maidenhair tree capsules provide a natural solution for improving liver function and reducing symptoms associated with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of cirrhosis.

Liver-protective compounds: The capsules contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to protect the liver from damage caused by toxins, viruses, and other pathogens, which is essential for managing cirrhosis.

Improvement in blood flow: Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood circulation can help increase oxygen delivery to damaged liver tissue, promoting healing and reducing fibrosis (scarring) associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids that help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.
Barks: Contains tannins and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in managing liver damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in autumn when they are mature and dry.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 8 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Crush dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, then mix with gelatin capsules (500mg) in a ratio of 1:3.
Fill the mixture into gelatin capsules using a capsule machine and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain saponins, bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve liver function and reduce inflammation. These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress. Additionally, sage has been found to modulate the immune response and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, leading to a reduction in fibrosis and scarring associated with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage capsules contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to the progression of cirrhosis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis associated with cirrhosis, promoting a healthier liver environment.

Antifibrotic properties: Herbal sage capsules may contain compounds that can inhibit collagen synthesis and deposition in the liver, helping to prevent or slow down liver scarring and fibrosis caused by cirrhosis.

Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, potentially benefiting cirrhotic patients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of salvia officinalis containing 4-6% essential oil content from local suppliers or online retailers.
Grind the dried leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes to release active compounds.
Mix 500 milligrams of ground salvia officinalis with 1000 milligrams of vitamin e powder in a small bowl to enhance bioavailability.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the salvia officinalis-vitamin e mixture using a capsule filler device for precise dosing.
Store the prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with cirrhosis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the liver and reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom of this disease. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to improve liver function and promote the regeneration of damaged liver tissue, making it a natural and effective way to manage cirrhosis symptoms and slow down its progression.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells, thus protecting the liver from further harm and promoting its natural repair processes, which may slow down or even reverse cirrhotic damage.

Soothing and calming effect: Chamomile's gentle sedative properties can help alleviate anxiety, stress, and sleep disturbances associated with cirrhosis, allowing patients to rest more comfortably and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are used due to their high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that helps reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cirrhosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture and preserve the herb.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, measuring out 1000mg of the powder per capsule.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powdered matricaria chamomilla, carefully sealing each capsule to prevent contamination and oxidation.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to one year, labeling them clearly with contents and date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with cirrhosis because of its ability to promote liver health and alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the liver from further damage and promote its natural healing processes.

Lithospermic acid: This compound has been shown to have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help combat viral hepatitis (a common cause of cirrhosis) and modulate the immune response to reduce liver inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a rich concentration of bioactive compounds, such as hypericin and flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers from a trusted source or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for at least three days or use a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered hypericum perforatum with 100 grams of silica gel to create an herbal blend and fill empty #0 gelatin capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce liver inflammation and scarring, which are hallmark features of cirrhosis. remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly.

Grind the dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the ground echinacea angustifolia root powder with 1000mg of silica filler and 50mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill empty #00 gelatin capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to promote liver health. The primary herb, figwort, has been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder issues, while other ingredients like dandelion root and milk thistle seed support the liver's natural detoxification processes. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scrophularia nodosa roots and dried them in a warm oven for 2 hours at 150°f.
Grind the dried scrophularia nodosa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered scrophularia nodosa with 250mg of slippery elm bark in a small bowl.
Fill #0 empty capsules with the scrophularia nodosa and slippery elm mixture, making sure each capsule contains an equal dose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with cirrhosis because they provide a natural and gentle soothing effect on the liver. Mix 250 milliliters of water with 100 grams of the powdered gentiana lutea and simmer for 30 minutes.

Fill empty capsules with the filtered herbal extract and seal immediately, storing them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with cirrhosis because they contain saponins, a unique compound that has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, fenugreek's ability to stimulate bile production and improve gut motility can also aid in reducing the buildup of toxins in the liver, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits for individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_properties: Galactomannan: Fenugreek contains a type of fiber called galactomannan, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with cirrhosis by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Trigonelline: Trigonelline is a compound found in fenugreek that has been shown to have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to cirrhosis development.

Saponins: Saponins are a type of phytochemical present in fenugreek that have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antifibrotic effects, which may help to reduce liver fibrosis (scarring) and improve liver function in individuals with cirrhosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate liver damage associated with cirrhosis.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds contain mucilages and fiber that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the liver, making them effective for managing cirrhotic liver disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh trigonella foenum graecum leaves and stems from the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material in a shaded area at room temperature for 7 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered trigonella foenum graecum with 50 milliliters of distilled water to create an herbal extract solution.
Fill #0 capsules with 250 milligrams each of the trigonella foenum graecum extract solution and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for cirrhosis are natural supplements that contain a blend of herbs, botanicals, and other plant-based extracts that help alleviate symptoms and slow down the progression of liver damage caused by cirrhosis. These capsules may include ingredients such as turmeric, milk thistle, and licorice root, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce liver inflammation and scarring. By taking these herbal capsules regularly, individuals with cirrhosis can experience improved digestion, reduced fatigue, and enhanced quality of life. For instance, a person with advanced cirrhosis may find it easier to manage their condition with the help of herbal supplements, allowing them to resume normal activities and spend more time with loved ones.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cirrhosis with herbal capsules is to focus on reducing liver inflammation and promoting regeneration. Turmeric's curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties, while milk thistle's silymarin helps protect the liver from damage. Other herbs like dandelion root, licorice root, and artichoke extract support liver function and detoxification. Combine these capsules with a balanced diet and lifestyle changes to effectively manage cirrhosis symptoms and promote overall health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cirrhosis that are treated with herbal capsules are fatty liver disease, chronic hepatitis, and liver fibrosis. Certain herbs like Milk Thistle, Turmeric, and Silymarin have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation, protect the liver from damage, and promote liver regeneration. Other herbal blends may include ingredients like Dandelion root, Licorice root, and Ginger to support digestive health and overall liver function.
